mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force earnings downgrades and cause negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following industrial production declines are positive in > 58% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or falling federal funds rate creates a soft landing environment that supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized indicates severe demand weakness that compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of low-growth quarters, or the threshold effect shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample classification tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels directly correlate with increased corporate output and revenue generation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the signal remains below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, or 5-day forward returns show no directional bias relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate during a late-cycle expansion signals wage pressure that accelerates inflation expectations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in tighter policy.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 57% of late-cycle unemployment declines, or the signal fails to separate from baseline returns in regime-filtered backtests.
